title: Face-Frame Buffet Table
summary: A face-frame buffet cabinet with a center divider, flat-panel doors, and a planked top, built almost entirely with pocket-hole joinery.
description:
A buffet or sideboard needs to look like a cabinet from the front and hide plywood everywhere else, which is exactly what a face frame does — it's a solid-wood frame applied to a plywood carcass, so every visible edge is real wood while the structure underneath is sheet goods.

The **center divider splits the interior into two independent sections**, which keeps each door's opening a manageable size and gives the case a middle post to anchor the face frame to — without it, a cabinet this wide would need a much heavier door or open awkwardly.

Leave a consistent **1/8" gap around every door opening.** Doors that fit too tight will bind the moment the house's humidity shifts the wood even slightly; a uniform small gap reads as intentional and still closes flush.

tools.note: Rips the 2x4 and 2x6 stock down to width for the frames and top.

materials.name: Wood screws, 3-1/2"
materials.note: Attaches the top through the face frame.

steps.title: Build the two face frames
steps.body: Rip the 2x4 stock to 3in wide and build two face frames — front and back — with pocket holes, glue, and pocket screws.

steps.title: Build the side panels
steps.body: Build two matching side panels from 3/4" plywood, with pocket holes down the back edge and the front edge trimmed with 1x4 stock.

steps.title: Assemble the case body
steps.body: Attach the two side panels to the back face frame, flush at the top on both sides. Cut a 3/4" plywood bottom panel and attach it to the back face frame and sides with pocket screws and glue.

steps.title: Add the center divider and front frame
steps.body: Cut a center divider slightly shorter than the sides and attach it to the base and the face frame's center leg. Attach the front face frame to the sides and divider, keeping the outside edges and top flush.

steps.title: Build the doors
steps.body: Build two flat-panel doors from 1/2" plywood, leaving a consistent 1/8in gap around each opening, and trim each door's face with mitered strips.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Rip the 2x6 boards to 5in wide and join them edge-to-edge with pocket holes, then attach the assembled top to the cabinet with long wood screws through the face frame.

steps.title: Back, finish, and hang the doors
steps.body: Cut a 1/4" plywood back panel and attach it with glue and staples or nails. Paint the cabinet and doors, stain the top, then install the hinges and door pulls.
